title: Sloanea rhodantha
taxonomy: Sloanea rhodantha belongs to the family Ebenaceae, which is a family of flowering plants. This family is a part of the larger clade of flowering plants known as angiosperms. More specifically, Sloanea rhodantha is a species of flowering plant in the order Celastrales. The classification of Sloanea rhodantha is further grouped under the class Magnoliopsida, which is a subclass of the larger class of flowering plants known as Dicotyledonae.

intro: Sloanea rhodantha, also known as the red-flowered Sloanea, is a plant species native to tropical regions, belonging to the family Erythroxylaceae. The Sloanea rhodantha is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat various health conditions such as fever, cough, and inflammation, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Sloanea rhodantha include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Sloanea rhodantha is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, or processed into capsules or tablets, to treat a range of health conditions, including respiratory and gastrointestinal disorders.
